# PumpKit

> Open-source TypeScript framework for real-time PumpFun monitoring on Solana.

PumpKit tracks on-chain PumpFun activity — fee claims, whale trades, token launches, and graduations — and delivers real-time alerts to Telegram channels, webhooks, and a live web dashboard.

## What is PumpKit?

PumpKit is a modular, open-source framework built in TypeScript for monitoring pump.fun activity on the Solana blockchain. It provides real-time event detection, enrichment with social data (GitHub profiles, X/Twitter followers), and multi-channel delivery (Telegram, Discord, Slack, webhooks).

### Key Capabilities

- **Fee Claim Monitoring**: Track PumpFun social fee claims per token, including claimer wallet, SOL amount, GitHub handle, and lifetime totals.
- **Token Launch Detection**: Instant alerts when new tokens are created on pump.fun, with social links, GitHub connections, cashback, and mayhem badges.
- **Whale Trade Tracking**: Monitor large buys and sells with market cap, bonding curve progress, and creator fee information.
- **Graduation Alerts**: Know the moment a token migrates from pump.fun to Raydium, with pool address and migration fee.
- **Fee Distribution Visibility**: See exactly how fees are split across shareholders, including basis point (BPS) breakdowns.
- **CTO (Community Takeover) Tracking**: Track creator wallet sales and community takeover events.
- **Live Web Dashboard**: Real-time event feed with filtering, search, and statistics.

## Packages

PumpKit is composed of 5 modular npm packages:

### @pumpkit/core (v0.3.2)
Shared framework: Grammy-based bot scaffolding, Solana WebSocket/HTTP monitors, program account decoders (pump.fun, Raydium), file-based + SQLite storage, structured logging (pino), health-check server.

### @pumpkit/monitor (v1.0.0)
All-in-one monitor: fee claims, launches, graduations, whale trades, CTO alerts. Includes REST API + SSE streaming. Configurable whale threshold, mayhem filter, webhook support. Railway-ready Docker image.

### @pumpkit/channel (v0.2.1)
Read-only Telegram channel bot. Broadcasts launch, trade, graduation, and claim events with customizable Markdown templates. Zero interaction, pure broadcast. Rate limiting with queue.

### @pumpkit/claim (v0.1.8)
Telegram bot for PumpFun fee claim lookups by token CA or creator handle. Commands: `/claim <CA>`, `/creator <handle>`. Includes CSV export and leaderboard.

### @pumpkit/tracker (v0.1.0)
Tracks watched wallets and tokens. Alerts on threshold-crossing trades, creator sells, and graduation. Personal watchlist per Telegram user.

## Technical Details

- **Language**: TypeScript (Node.js)
- **Blockchain**: Solana (Mainnet Beta)
- **Protocol**: pump.fun (PumpFun)
- **DEX**: Raydium (graduation target)
- **Bot Framework**: Grammy (Telegram)
- **Web**: React + Vite + Tailwind CSS
- **Streaming**: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- **Deployment**: Docker, Railway
- **License**: MIT
- **Repository**: https://github.com/nirholas/pumpkit

## Links

- Website: https://pumpkit.dev
- Documentation: https://pumpkit.dev/docs
- Live Dashboard: https://pumpkit.dev/dashboard
- Packages: https://pumpkit.dev/packages
- GitHub: https://github.com/nirholas/pumpkit
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/org/pumpkit

## FAQ

### What is pump.fun?
Pump.fun is a Solana-based platform for creating and trading meme tokens with a bonding curve mechanism. Tokens can "graduate" to Raydium once they reach a certain market cap threshold.

### What are PumpFun fee claims?
When tokens are created on pump.fun with a GitHub account linked, the creator earns social fees from trading activity. These fees can be claimed on-chain.

### How does PumpKit connect to Solana?
PumpKit uses WebSocket subscriptions and HTTP polling against Solana RPC endpoints (with automatic fallback) to detect on-chain events in real time.

### Is PumpKit free?
Yes. PumpKit is open-source under the MIT license. All packages are free to use.

### Can I self-host PumpKit?
Yes. PumpKit includes a Dockerfile and Railway configuration for easy self-hosting. You can also run it locally with Node.js.
